title: rubus cymophorus
intro: Rubus cymophorus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a type of wild raspberry species native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus cymophorus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Rubus cymophorus is often prepared as a tea, syrup, or tincture, and can also be used in poultices and salves for topical application.
